return.en: go back to a place or state

ex.en: She returned home late last night.

return.en: give something back to its owner or original place

ex.en: I need to return this book to the library.
ex.ko: 나는 이 책을 도서관에 반납해야 한다.

return.en: a journey to a place and back again

ex.en: I bought a return ticket to London.

return.en: the profit gained from an investment or business

ex.en: The company reported strong returns this year.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 올해 높은 수익을 기록했다.

reunion.en: the act of meeting again after being apart

ex.en: Their reunion after ten years was very emotional.

reunion.en: a party where former classmates, friends, or family members meet again

ex.en: We are having a family reunion this weekend.
ex.ko: 우리는 이번 주말에 가족 모임을 갖는다.

reveal.en: make something visible that was hidden

ex.en: She pulled back the curtain to reveal a hidden room.
ex.ko: 그녀는 커튼을 젖혀 숨겨진 방을 드러냈다.

reveal.en: make known information that was secret

ex.en: He finally revealed the truth about the accident.
ex.ko: 그는 마침내 사고에 대한 진실을 밝혔다.

rhythm.en: a regular repeated pattern of sound or movement

ex.en: The song has a great rhythm for dancing.
ex.ko: 그 노래는 춤추기 좋은 리듬을 가지고 있다.

right-hand.en: on or towards the right side

ex.en: Take the right-hand turn at the crossroads.

right-hand.en: most helpful and trusted, especially in 'right-hand man'

ex.en: He is the manager's right-hand man.

road.en: a way built for vehicles to travel on

ex.en: We drove down a long, winding road.
ex.ko: 우리는 길고 구불구불한 도로를 따라 운전했다.

road.en: a way or course of action leading to something

ex.en: It was a long road to recovery.

roast.en: of meat, cooked using dry heat in an oven

ex.en: We had roast chicken for dinner.

roast.en: cook food using dry heat, usually in an oven

ex.en: She roasted the vegetables with olive oil.

rob.en: take money or property from a person or place illegally, often using force

ex.en: Thieves robbed the bank yesterday.

robbery.en: the crime of stealing from a person or place using force or threats

ex.en: The robbery took place in broad daylight.

robin.en: a small bird with a reddish breast

ex.en: A robin landed on the fence.

roll.en: move by turning over and over

ex.en: The ball rolled down the hill.

roll.en: wrap something into the shape of a cylinder

ex.en: He rolled up the yoga mat.

roll.en: a small, round piece of bread

ex.en: She bought a bag of fresh rolls.
ex.ko: 그녀는 신선한 롤빵 한 봉지를 샀다.

ex.en: The teacher called the roll before class.

romance.en: a relationship between two people who are in love

ex.en: Their romance began in college.

romance.en: a story about a romantic relationship

ex.en: She loves reading historical romances.
ex.ko: 그녀는 역사 로맨스 소설 읽기를 좋아한다.

romantic.en: relating to or expressing feelings of love

ex.en: They had a romantic dinner by candlelight.
ex.ko: 그들은 촛불 아래에서 낭만적인 저녁 식사를 했다.

romantic.en: not sensible or realistic about practical matters

ex.en: His romantic view of farming ignored the hard work involved.
ex.ko: 농사에 대한 그의 이상주의적인 시각은 힘든 노동을 간과했다.

roof.en: the covering on top of a building or vehicle

ex.en: Snow covered the roof of the house.

roof.en: an upper limit on an amount, such as prices or spending

ex.en: The government set a roof on rent increases.

root.en: the part of a plant that grows underground

ex.en: The tree's roots spread deep into the soil.
ex.ko: 그 나무의 뿌리는 땅속 깊이 뻗어 있다.

root.en: the origin or cause of something

ex.en: They tried to find the root of the problem.

root.en: cheer for a team or person to succeed

ex.en: We were rooting for our local team.
ex.ko: 우리는 우리 지역 팀을 응원하고 있었다.

rope.en: a thick strong cord made of twisted fibers

ex.en: They tied the boat with a rope.
The trip takes roughly two hours.
ex.ko: 그 여행은 대략 두 시간 걸린다.

roughly.en: in a rough or violent way

ex.en: He pushed the door roughly and it broke.
ex.ko: 그는 문을 거칠게 밀었고 문이 부서졌다.

round.en: turn or move around a central point, or make something circular

ex.en: The wheels round smoothly on the track.
The table is round.

round.en: one of a series of contests or parts of a game

ex.en: She won the first round of the competition.
ex.ko: 그녀는 대회 첫 번째 라운드에서 이겼다.

round.en: in a circle or curve around something

ex.en: They walked round the lake.

round.en: adjust a number to the nearest whole number, ten, etc.

ex.en: Round the price up to the nearest dollar.
ex.ko: 가격을 가장 가까운 달러 단위로 반올림해라.

round.en: a single shot or unit of ammunition fired from a gun

ex.en: He fired a round into the air.
ex.ko: 그는 공중에 탄환 한 발을 쐈다.

roundabout.en: a place where roads meet, arranged as a circle that traffic goes around

ex.en: Turn right at the roundabout.

roundabout.en: a large rotating machine with seats for children to ride (British English)

ex.en: The kids rode the roundabout at the fair.
He gave a roundabout answer to avoid the truth.
ex.ko: 그는 진실을 피하려고 에두른 답을 했다.

royal.en: relating to a king or queen or their family

ex.en: The royal family waved to the crowd.

rugby.en: a team sport played with an oval-shaped ball

ex.en: He plays rugby every Saturday.

ruin.en: the remains of a building that has been destroyed or is falling apart

ex.en: They explored the ancient ruins.
The rain ruined our picnic.

ruin.en: cause someone to lose all their money

ex.en: The bad investment ruined the company.

rumor.en: information or a story that people talk about but is not proven true

ex.en: There's a rumor that the shop is closing.
ex.ko: 그 가게가 문을 닫는다는 소문이 있다.

rumor.en: spread unproven information as if it were true

ex.en: It was rumored that she had resigned.

runner.en: a person who runs, especially in a race

ex.en: The runner crossed the finish line first.
ex.ko: 그 주자는 결승선을 가장 먼저 통과했다.

runner.en: a player who is on or running to a base in baseball

ex.en: The runner stole second base.

running.en: the activity or sport of running

ex.en: Running is good exercise.

running.en: of a machine or system, working or operating

ex.en: Leave the engine running while you wait.
There's a running joke about his bad cooking.
ex.ko: 그의 형편없는 요리 실력에 대한 계속되는 농담이 있다.

rush.en: move or do something very quickly, especially because of limited time

ex.en: We had to rush to catch the train.
ex.ko: 우리는 기차를 타기 위해 서둘러야 했다.

rush.en: a sudden movement of many people or things to one place

ex.en: There was a rush of customers during the sale.

rush.en: attack or move towards suddenly and quickly

ex.en: The fans rushed the stage after the concert.
ex.ko: 콘서트가 끝난 후 팬들이 무대로 돌진했다.

sadly.en: used to say that something is unfortunate

ex.en: Sadly, the shop has closed down.
She looked at the photo sadly.

safe.en: free from danger or risk of harm

ex.en: Make sure the children are safe.

safe.en: a strong box used to keep valuable things secure

ex.en: He kept his jewelry in a safe.

sailing.en: the activity or sport of traveling in a boat using sails

ex.en: They went sailing on the lake.

sake.en: the purpose or reason for doing something, mainly used in 'for the sake of'

ex.en: She quit smoking for the sake of her health.

sake.en: a Japanese alcoholic drink made from fermented rice

ex.en: They drank warm sake with dinner.
ex.ko: 그들은 저녁 식사와 함께 따뜻한 사케를 마셨다.

salt.en: a white substance used to flavor or preserve food

ex.en: Add a pinch of salt to the soup.

sample.en: a small part of something that shows what the whole is like

ex.en: Can I have a free sample of the perfume?
ex.ko: 그 향수 무료 샘플 받을 수 있나요?

sample.en: try a small amount of something to see what it is like

ex.en: We sampled several cheeses at the market.

sandy.en: covered with or consisting of sand

ex.en: We walked along the sandy beach.

sandy.en: of hair, light yellowish-brown in color

ex.en: He has sandy hair and freckles.
ex.ko: 그는 연한 갈색 머리에 주근깨가 있다.

satisfy.en: make someone pleased by giving them what they want or need

ex.en: The meal really satisfied my hunger.
ex.ko: 그 식사는 내 허기를 정말 만족시켰다.

ex.en: Applicants must satisfy all the eligibility criteria.
ex.ko: 지원자는 모든 자격 요건을 충족해야 한다.

sauce.en: a thick liquid added to food to give it more flavor

ex.en: She poured tomato sauce over the pasta.

sausage.en: a food made of minced meat stuffed into a casing

ex.en: We grilled sausages for breakfast.

scale.en: a device used to weigh things

ex.en: She stepped on the bathroom scale.

scale.en: the size or extent of something

ex.en: The scale of the disaster was huge.

scale.en: the ratio between a map or model and the real size of something

ex.en: The map has a scale of one to fifty thousand.

scale.en: climb to the top of something

ex.en: The climbers scaled the cliff.

scale.en: one of the small flat pieces that cover the skin of fish or reptiles

ex.en: The fish's scales shimmered in the sun.

scarf.en: a piece of cloth worn around the neck or head for warmth or style

ex.en: She wrapped a warm scarf around her neck.

scene.en: a part of a film, play, or story that shows a particular action or place

ex.en: That was the funniest scene in the movie.
ex.ko: 그게 그 영화에서 가장 웃긴 장면이었다.

ex.en: Police arrived quickly at the scene of the crime.

scene.cue: 감정을 크게 드러내는 소동 (make a scene)
scene.en: a public display of strong emotion or anger

ex.en: He made a scene at the restaurant.

scenery.en: the natural features of a landscape, especially when beautiful

ex.en: The mountain scenery was breathtaking.
ex.ko: 그 산의 경치는 숨이 막힐 정도였다.

schedule.en: arrange for something to happen at a particular time

ex.en: The meeting is scheduled for 3 p.m.

schedule.en: a plan of things to do and when to do them

ex.en: I have a busy schedule this week.
Schoolchildren crossed the street carefully.

schoolwork.en: work that students do for school, such as homework

ex.en: She always finishes her schoolwork before dinner.
ex.ko: 그녀는 항상 저녁 식사 전에 숙제를 끝낸다.

scientific.en: relating to or based on science

ex.en: They used a scientific method to test the theory.
ex.ko: 그들은 이론을 검증하기 위해 과학적인 방법을 사용했다.

scissors.en: a tool with two blades used for cutting

ex.en: Use the scissors to cut along the line.

scream.en: make a loud, sharp cry, especially from fear or pain

ex.en: She screamed when she saw the spider.
They heard a scream from the house next door.

screen.en: test or examine someone or something for a particular purpose

ex.en: The hospital screens all newborns for hearing problems.
ex.ko: 그 병원은 모든 신생아의 청력 문제를 검사한다.

screen.en: the flat surface on a device or in a cinema where images or text appear

ex.en: He stared at the computer screen all day.
ex.ko: 그는 하루 종일 컴퓨터 화면을 응시했다.

screen.en: an object used to block light, view, or something unwanted

ex.en: They put a screen around the hospital bed for privacy.
ex.ko: 그들은 사생활 보호를 위해 병상 주위에 가리개를 세웠다.

ex.en: She screwed the shelf to the wall.

screw.en: a small metal fastener with a spiral thread, used to join things

ex.en: He tightened the screw with a screwdriver.
The company screwed him out of his bonus.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 그를 속여 보너스를 주지 않았다.

script.en: the written words of a play, film, or broadcast

ex.en: The actors rehearsed with the script.

script.en: a particular system of writing used for a language

ex.en: Arabic script is written from right to left.

seafood.en: fish and shellfish eaten as food

ex.en: The restaurant is famous for its fresh seafood.

search.en: try to find something by looking carefully

ex.en: I searched the whole house for my keys.
ex.ko: 나는 열쇠를 찾으려고 온 집 안을 뒤졌다.

search.en: the act of looking for something

ex.en: The search for the missing dog continued all night.
ex.ko: 잃어버린 개를 찾는 수색이 밤새 계속되었다.

search.en: examine someone's body, bags, or property to find something hidden

ex.en: Security searched his bag at the airport.

seaside.en: an area next to the sea, especially as a place for a vacation

ex.en: We spent the weekend at the seaside.

secret.en: not known or seen by other people

ex.en: He has a secret plan to surprise her.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀를 놀라게 할 비밀 계획이 있다.

ex.en: Can you keep a secret?

secretary.en: a person who does office work such as typing letters and organizing schedules for someone

ex.en: Her secretary scheduled all her meetings.
ex.ko: 그녀의 비서가 모든 회의 일정을 잡았다.

secretary.en: the head of a government department, especially in the US

ex.en: The Secretary of State met with foreign leaders.

seed.en: a small hard part of a plant from which a new plant can grow

ex.en: She planted the seeds in the garden.
Farmers seed the fields in early spring.
ex.ko: 농부들은 이른 봄에 밭에 씨를 뿌린다.

seed.en: one of the top players in a tournament, ranked to avoid early matches against each other

ex.en: She is the top seed in this year's tournament.

seek.en: try to find or obtain something

ex.en: She is seeking a new job.

seem.en: give the impression of being something, without being certain

ex.en: She seems tired today.

semester.en: one of the two main periods that a school or university year is divided into

ex.en: The fall semester starts in September.

send.en: cause something to go or be taken to another place

ex.en: I sent her a birthday card.

send.en: cause someone to go somewhere for a particular purpose

ex.en: The company sent him to Tokyo for training.
ex.ko: 회사는 그를 연수를 위해 도쿄로 파견했다.

ex.en: She was promoted to a senior manager position.
He is senior to me by five years.

senior.en: a student in the final year of high school or college (US)

ex.en: She is a senior at the university this year.

sense.en: become aware of something without being told directly

ex.en: She sensed that something was wrong.

sense.en: one of the natural abilities (sight, hearing, smell, taste, touch) used to perceive the world

ex.en: Dogs have a strong sense of smell.

sense.en: the meaning of a word or expression

ex.en: The word has several senses.
ex.ko: 그 단어는 여러 의미를 가지고 있다.

sense.en: good, practical judgment, or the quality of being reasonable

ex.en: It makes sense to save money.

separate.en: divide something into parts or move things apart

ex.en: Separate the egg whites from the yolks.

separate.en: not joined or connected to something else

ex.en: They sleep in separate rooms.

separate.en: stop living together as a couple or end a relationship

ex.en: They separated after ten years of marriage.
Please take my advice seriously.
She was seriously injured in the accident.

serve.en: give food or drink to someone

ex.en: The waiter served us dinner.

serve.en: work for a person, organization, or country

ex.en: He served in the army for five years.

serve.en: have a particular purpose or function

ex.en: This room serves as an office.

serve.en: hit the ball to start play in tennis or a similar sport

ex.en: She served an ace to win the match.

several.en: more than two or three, but not very many

ex.en: I've read several books this month.
ex.ko: 나는 이번 달에 책을 여러 권 읽었다.

sew.en: join or repair cloth using a needle and thread

ex.en: My grandmother sewed a button on my shirt.
ex.ko: 할머니가 내 셔츠에 단추를 꿰매 주셨다.

shade.en: an area protected from direct sunlight

ex.en: We sat in the shade of a tree.

shade.en: a particular version of a color, lighter or darker than another

ex.en: She painted the wall a lighter shade of blue.
ex.ko: 그녀는 벽을 더 옅은 색조의 파란색으로 칠했다.

shade.en: block light or protect something from direct sunlight

ex.en: He shaded his eyes with his hand.

shadow.en: secretly follow a person to watch what they do

ex.en: The detective shadowed the suspect all day.
ex.ko: 그 형사는 하루 종일 용의자를 미행했다.

shadow.en: a dark shape made when something blocks light

ex.en: His shadow stretched across the wall.

shadow.en: an area of darkness where light is blocked

ex.en: The garden was full of shadows at dusk.

shall.en: used to talk about the future, or to make a formal offer or suggestion

ex.en: I shall return tomorrow.

shampoo.en: a liquid soap used for washing hair

ex.en: She bought a new bottle of shampoo.
ex.ko: 그녀는 새 샴푸 한 병을 샀다.

ex.en: He shampooed his hair every morning.
ex.ko: 그는 매일 아침 샴푸로 머리를 감았다.

shape.en: influence or form the development of something

ex.en: Her childhood experiences shaped her career choices.
ex.ko: 그녀의 어린 시절 경험이 진로 선택에 영향을 주었다.

shape.en: the outer form or outline of something

ex.en: The clouds formed the shape of a heart.

shape.en: the physical condition of someone's body

ex.en: He exercises daily to stay in shape.
ex.ko: 그는 몸 상태를 유지하려고 매일 운동한다.

shell.en: attack a place with artillery shells

ex.en: The city was shelled for three days.

shell.en: the hard outer covering of an egg, nut, or shellfish

ex.en: She cracked the egg shell carefully.

shell.en: a metal case filled with explosive, fired from a large gun

ex.en: An unexploded shell was found in the field.

shine.en: give off or reflect light brightly

ex.en: The sun shone brightly all day.

shine.en: be very good at something, especially in a noticeable way

ex.en: She really shines in math class.
ex.ko: 그녀는 수학 수업에서 정말 두각을 나타낸다.

shock.cue: 예상 못 한 일로 놀라게 하다
shock.en: surprise someone very much, usually in an unpleasant way

ex.en: The news shocked everyone in the office.
ex.ko: 그 소식은 사무실의 모든 사람에게 충격을 주었다.

shock.en: a strong feeling of surprise and distress

ex.en: She was still in shock after the accident.
ex.ko: 그녀는 사고 후에도 여전히 충격에 빠져 있었다.

shock.en: a sudden painful feeling caused by electricity passing through the body

ex.en: He got a shock from the faulty wire.

shoot.en: fire a gun or other weapon at someone or something

ex.en: The hunter shot at the deer.

shoot.en: make a film or take photographs

ex.en: They shot the movie in New Zealand.

shoot.en: kick, hit, or throw a ball toward a goal to try to score

ex.en: She shot the ball into the basket.
The car shot past us on the highway.
ex.ko: 그 차가 고속도로에서 우리를 쏜살같이 지나갔다.

shop assistant.en: a person who works in a shop helping customers

ex.en: The shop assistant helped me find the right size.

shore.en: the land along the edge of the sea, a lake, or a river

ex.en: We walked along the shore at sunset.

shot.en: an act of firing a gun

ex.en: We heard a shot in the distance.

shot.en: a photograph, or a scene filmed by a camera

ex.en: That's a great shot of the sunset.

shot.en: an attempt or opportunity to do something

ex.en: Give it a shot, you might succeed.

shot.en: an attempt to score in a sport by kicking, hitting, or throwing the ball

ex.en: His shot hit the crossbar.

shot.cue: 주사 한 대; 소량의 독한 술 한 잔
shot.en: an injection of medicine, or a small strong alcoholic drink

ex.en: The doctor gave her a flu shot.

shout.en: say something in a loud voice

ex.en: He shouted for help.
We heard a shout from the crowd.

shut.en: close something, such as a door or window

ex.en: Please shut the door behind you.
The shop was shut when we arrived.
ex.ko: 우리가 도착했을 때 가게는 닫혀 있었다.

sightseeing.en: the activity of visiting interesting places as a tourist

ex.en: We went sightseeing around the old town.

significant.en: important enough to have a real effect or meaning

ex.en: This is a significant discovery for medicine.

significant.cue: 수치·양이 무시할 수 없을 만큼 큰
significant.en: large enough to be noticed or to matter

ex.en: There was a significant increase in sales.

silence.en: the state of not speaking or making any sound

ex.en: An awkward silence fell over the room.
The silence of the forest was calming.

silence.en: make someone stop talking or making noise

ex.en: The teacher raised her hand to silence the class.
ex.ko: 선생님은 손을 들어 학생들을 조용히 시켰다.

ex.en: She silently closed the door.
It was silly of me to forget my keys.

silly.en: amusing in a playful or childish way

ex.en: He made a silly face to make the baby laugh.
ex.ko: 그는 아기를 웃기려고 우스꽝스러운 표정을 지었다.

ex.en: She wore a silver necklace.

silver.en: having the shiny grey-white color of silver

ex.en: He drives a silver car.

silver.en: a medal awarded for second place in a competition

ex.en: She won silver at the Olympics.

similar.en: having the same or nearly the same characteristics as something else

ex.en: Her dress is similar to mine.

simple.en: easy to understand or do, not complicated

ex.en: The instructions were simple to follow.

simple.en: plain, without unnecessary decoration or extra things

ex.en: They live a simple life in the countryside.
Please explain it simply.
I simply want to help.

since.en: from a particular time in the past until now

ex.en: I haven't seen him since last year.
ex.ko: 나는 작년 이후로 그를 보지 못했다.

since.en: used to give the reason for something

ex.en: Since it's raining, let's stay home.
She took singing lessons for years.
ex.ko: 그녀는 몇 년 동안 노래 레슨을 받았다.

single.en: a hit in baseball that allows the batter to reach first base safely

ex.en: He hit a single to left field.
She has been single for two years.

single.en: only one; not more than one

ex.en: I didn't see a single mistake in his work.
ex.ko: 나는 그의 작업에서 실수를 단 하나도 보지 못했다.

singular.en: the grammatical form of a word used for one person or thing

ex.en: 'Cat' is the singular form of 'cats'.
He has a singular talent for languages.
ex.ko: 그는 언어에 대한 특이한 재능이 있다.

sink.en: go down below the surface of water or another liquid

ex.en: The boat began to sink slowly.

sink.en: a fixed basin with taps, used for washing dishes or hands

ex.en: She washed the dishes in the sink.

sitting room.en: a room in a house used for relaxing and sitting

ex.en: We watched TV in the sitting room.

situation.en: the set of circumstances that exist at a particular time and place

ex.en: We need to think carefully about the situation.
ex.ko: 우리는 이 상황에 대해 신중히 생각해야 한다.

situation.en: a job or position in an organization (formal)

ex.en: He applied for a situation at the local bank.

skate.en: move along on skates, especially on ice

ex.en: They skated on the frozen lake.

skate.en: a boot with a blade or wheels attached, used for skating

ex.en: He put on his skates and stepped onto the ice.
ex.ko: 그는 스케이트를 신고 얼음 위로 나갔다.

skateboarding.en: the sport of riding on a skateboard

ex.en: Skateboarding is popular among teenagers.
ex.ko: 스케이트보드 타기는 십대들 사이에서 인기가 있다.

skating.en: the sport or activity of moving on skates

ex.en: We went skating at the indoor rink.
We skied down the mountain.

ski.en: one of a pair of long, narrow boards used for gliding over snow

ex.en: He waxed his skis before the race.
ex.ko: 그는 경주 전에 스키에 왁스를 발랐다.

skiing.en: the sport of moving over snow on skis

ex.en: Skiing is her favorite winter sport.
ex.ko: 스키 타기는 그녀가 가장 좋아하는 겨울 스포츠이다.

slave.cue: 다른 사람의 소유물처럼 강제로 부려지는 사람
slave.en: a person who is owned by another person and forced to work without pay

ex.en: The novel tells the story of a freed slave.
ex.ko: 그 소설은 해방된 노예의 이야기를 다룬다.

slave.en: someone who is completely controlled by a habit or influence

ex.en: He's a slave to his phone.

sleepless.en: unable to sleep, or without sleep

ex.en: She spent a sleepless night worrying about the exam.
ex.ko: 그녀는 시험 걱정에 잠 못 이루는 밤을 보냈다.

sleepy.en: feeling like you want or need to sleep

ex.en: I felt sleepy after lunch.

slice.en: cut something into thin flat pieces

ex.en: She sliced the bread for sandwiches.

slice.en: a thin flat piece cut from something larger

ex.en: He ate a slice of pizza.
The kids slid down the icy hill.

slide.en: a playground structure with a smooth sloped surface for children to slide down

ex.en: The children lined up for the slide.

slide.en: a single image or page in a presentation

ex.en: He added a chart to the next slide.

slim.en: having a thin, attractive body shape

ex.en: She has a slim figure.

slim.en: very small in amount or degree

ex.en: There is only a slim chance of winning.

slowly.en: at a slow speed; without hurrying

ex.en: He walked slowly to the door.

smooth.en: having an even surface, without bumps or roughness

ex.en: The baby's skin was soft and smooth.
We had a smooth flight to Tokyo.
The meeting went smoothly.

snack.en: a small amount of food eaten between meals

ex.en: I had a snack before dinner.
ex.ko: 나는 저녁 먹기 전에 간식을 먹었다.

snowboarding.cue: 스노보드로 눈 덮인 슬로프를 내려오는 스포츠
snowboarding.en: the sport of riding down a snow-covered slope on a snowboard

ex.en: He learned snowboarding last winter.
ex.ko: 그는 지난겨울에 스노보드 타는 법을 배웠다.

soap.cue: 몸이나 손을 씻을 때 쓰는 세정제
soap.en: a substance used with water for washing your body

ex.en: Wash your hands with soap and water.

society.en: a large group of people who live together in an organized community

ex.en: Technology has changed modern society.

society.en: an organization of people who share a common interest

ex.en: He joined the school's chess society.

sock.en: a piece of clothing worn on the foot, inside a shoe

ex.en: He put on a pair of clean socks.
ex.ko: 그는 깨끗한 양말 한 켤레를 신었다.

ex.en: He ordered a soda with his burger.

soft.en: not hard or firm; pleasant to touch

ex.en: The pillow is very soft.
She spoke in a soft voice.
He has a soft heart when it comes to animals.

soft drink.en: a cold, usually fizzy drink that contains no alcohol

ex.en: We drank soft drinks at the party.

softly.en: quietly or gently, without loud noise

ex.en: She spoke softly so as not to wake the baby.
ex.ko: 그녀는 아기를 깨우지 않으려고 조용히 말했다.

software.en: the programs used to operate a computer or device

ex.en: We need to update the software on this computer.
ex.ko: 우리는 이 컴퓨터의 소프트웨어를 업데이트해야 한다.

soldier.en: a person who serves in an army

ex.en: The soldier returned home after the war.
ex.ko: 그 군인은 전쟁이 끝난 후 집으로 돌아왔다.

solution.en: a way of solving a problem

ex.en: We found a good solution to the problem.
ex.ko: 우리는 그 문제에 대한 좋은 해결책을 찾았다.

solution.cue: 물질이 액체에 완전히 녹아 섞인 것
solution.en: a liquid mixture in which a substance is completely dissolved

ex.en: Mix salt and water to make a saline solution.
ex.ko: 소금과 물을 섞어 식염수 용액을 만들어라.

ex.en: Somebody left their umbrella here.

someday.en: at some unspecified time in the future

ex.en: I hope to visit Paris someday.

somewhere.en: in, at, or to a place that is not known or named

ex.en: I left my phone somewhere in the house.
ex.ko: 나는 휴대폰을 집 안 어딘가에 두었다.

ex.en: I heard a strange sound outside.

sound.en: based on good judgment or reasoning; reliable

ex.en: That's a sound piece of advice.

sound.en: give the impression of being something when heard

ex.en: That sounds like a great idea.

source.en: the place or thing something comes from

ex.en: Sunlight is a source of energy.

source.en: a person, document, or place that provides information

ex.en: She refused to reveal her source.

south.en: the direction that is opposite north

ex.en: The wind is blowing from the south.

south.en: situated in or facing the south

ex.en: They live in the south part of the city.
ex.ko: 그들은 그 도시의 남쪽 지역에 산다.

ex.en: We drove south for three hours.
ex.ko: 우리는 세 시간 동안 남쪽으로 운전했다.

space.cue: 물건을 둘 수 있는 빈 곳
space.en: an empty area that can be used for something

ex.en: There isn't enough space for another chair.
ex.ko: 의자를 하나 더 놓을 공간이 충분하지 않다.

space.en: the area outside the Earth's atmosphere where the stars and planets are

ex.en: Astronauts travel into space.

spaceship.en: a vehicle used for traveling in space

ex.en: The spaceship landed on the moon.

spaghetti.en: a type of pasta made in long, thin strings

ex.en: She cooked spaghetti with tomato sauce.
ex.ko: 그녀는 토마토 소스를 곁들인 스파게티를 요리했다.

speaker.en: a person who gives a speech to an audience

ex.en: The keynote speaker impressed everyone.
ex.ko: 그 기조 연설자는 모두에게 깊은 인상을 남겼다.

speaker.en: a device that produces sound from an electrical signal

ex.en: He connected his phone to the speaker.

specific.en: clearly defined or particular, not general

ex.en: Can you give me a specific example?
The car sped down the highway.
The train was traveling at high speed.

spill.en: accidentally let a liquid flow out of a container

ex.en: He spilled coffee on his shirt.

splash.en: make liquid fly around in drops, hitting something

ex.en: The kids splashed water at each other in the pool.

splash.en: the sound of liquid hitting something

ex.en: We heard a splash as the stone hit the water.
ex.ko: 돌이 물에 떨어지며 첨벙 소리가 났다.

split.en: divide or break into two or more parts

ex.en: The log split in two when it fell.
ex.ko: 그 통나무는 떨어질 때 둘로 갈라졌다.

split.en: share something between two or more people

ex.en: Let's split the bill.

split.en: a division or separation, especially between people

ex.en: There was a split in the party over the decision.
ex.ko: 그 결정을 두고 당내에 분열이 있었다.

spoon.en: a piece of cutlery with a small bowl-shaped end, used for eating or serving food

ex.en: She stirred her tea with a spoon.

sports center.cue: 다양한 운동 시설이 모여 있는 곳
sports center.en: a building with facilities for playing sports and exercising

ex.en: We go swimming at the sports center every week.
ex.ko: 우리는 매주 스포츠 센터에서 수영을 한다.

spread.en: open something out so it covers a larger area

ex.en: She spread the map on the table.

spread.en: become or make something known or present over a wider area

ex.en: The news spread quickly through town.
ex.ko: 그 소식은 마을 전체에 빠르게 퍼졌다.

spread.cue: 빵에 발라 먹는 잼·버터 같은 것
spread.en: a soft food, such as jam or butter, spread on bread

ex.en: She put some cheese spread on her toast.

square.en: a shape with four equal sides and four right angles

ex.en: Draw a square on the paper.

square.en: an open public area in a town, often surrounded by buildings

ex.en: Tourists gathered in the town square.

square.en: having the shape of a square

ex.en: The room has a square table.

stadium.en: a large structure with seats for watching sports events

ex.en: The stadium was full for the final match.

staff.en: the group of people who work for an organization

ex.en: All staff must wear name badges.

stair.cue: 위아래 층을 오르내리는 계단 한 칸
stair.en: one of a set of steps used to go up or down between floors

ex.en: She tripped on the top stair.
ex.ko: 그녀는 맨 위 계단에서 발을 헛디뎠다.

stamp.en: walk with heavy, forceful steps, often to show anger

ex.en: He stamped his feet in frustration.

stamp.en: a small piece of paper stuck on mail to show that postage has been paid

ex.en: She put a stamp on the envelope.

stamp.en: press an object onto a surface to leave a mark

ex.en: The officer stamped the document.
Please state your name and address.

state.en: one of the regions that a country such as the US is divided into

ex.en: California is a state in the US.

state.en: the condition someone or something is in at a particular time

ex.en: The house was in a poor state of repair.
ex.ko: 그 집은 수리가 안 된 상태였다.

state.en: a country considered as an organized political community

ex.en: The state provides free education.

statement.en: something that someone says or writes officially

ex.en: The company released a statement about the incident.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 그 사건에 대한 성명을 발표했다.

statement.en: a document showing details of money paid, received, or owed

ex.en: I checked my bank statement online.

statue.en: a sculpture made to look like a person or animal

ex.en: There is a statue of the king in the square.

steak.en: a thick slice of meat, usually beef

ex.en: He ordered a steak with fries.

steal.en: take something that belongs to someone else without permission

ex.en: Someone stole my bike last night.
ex.ko: 누군가 어젯밤에 내 자전거를 훔쳐 갔다.

steal.en: advance to the next base in baseball without the ball being hit

ex.en: He stole second base in the ninth inning.
